sub_pixel_system.notes: Not applicable - puzzle game with grid-based movement

special_mechanics.name: Grid-Based Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player moves one square at a time, either horizontally or vertically onto empty floor squares. No continuous movement or physics-based locomotion.

special_mechanics.name: Box Pushing
special_mechanics.description: Player can walk up to a box and push it to an empty square directly beyond it. Boxes can only be pushed, never pulled. Only one box can be pushed at a time. If a box is pushed against a wall or another box, it does not move.

special_mechanics.name: Collision and Blocking
special_mechanics.description: Boxes and walls block player movement. Boxes become trapped if pushed into deadlock situations against walls or other boxes.

tier_notes: Tier 3 (T3) - Empirical/qualitative data only. Super Sokoban is a puzzle game with grid-based mechanics, not a physics-based platformer. No continuous movement physics, gravity, or jump mechanics apply. Player movement is discrete (one square per input) and collision-based on the tile grid.
